points.text: CRISPR Therapeutics (Nasdaq: CRSP) confirmed senior management participation at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Global Healthcare Conference on May 12, 2026, highlighting its portfolio including CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel), the world's first approved CRISPR-based therapy for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.

Caribou Biosciences (Nasdaq: CRBU), a clinical-stage CRISPR genome-editing biopharmaceutical company, announced management participation in the H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual BioConnect Investor Conference on May 19, 2026, to discuss its chRDNA genome-editing technology and pipeline.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR genome editing systems — In vivo gene editing pipeline — Announced pricing of up to $319.4 million public offering to fund development of transformative medicines for serious diseases.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR technology — Patent portfolio — Holds exclusive license to Broad Institute's Cas12a patent estate and Broad Institute/Harvard University's Cas9 patent estates for human medicines.

Verve Therapeutics — VERVE-102 — Genetic hypercholesterolemia — In vivo base editing (CRISPR-derived technology) achieved up to 88% reduction in PCSK9 and 62% reduction in LDL cholesterol at highest dose in Heart-2 study.

Caribou Biosciences (Nasdaq: CRBU) operates as a clinical-stage CRISPR genome-editing biopharmaceutical company developing vispacabtagene regedleucel (vispa-cel) and CB-011 as off-the-shelf CAR-T cell therapies for hematologic malignancies.
Editas Medicine (Nasdaq: EDIT) is a pioneering gene editing company focused on translating CRISPR genome editing systems into a robust pipeline of transformative in vivo medicines.

Verve Therapeutics — VERVE-102 — Genetic high cholesterol — In vivo base editing therapy demonstrated up to 88% reduction in PCSK9 and 62% reduction in LDL cholesterol at highest dose in Heart-2 study.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease / Transfusion-Dependent Beta Thalassemia — World's first approved CRISPR-based therapy; company advancing SyNTase™ editing platform for precise gene correction.

Caribou Biosciences — vispacabtagene regedleucel (vispa-cel) — Hematologic Malignancies — Clinical-stage off-the-shelf CAR-T cell therapy utilizing chRDNA genome-editing technology for armored cell therapy.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR-based pipeline — In vivo gene editing — Secured $319.4 million via public offering to advance precision gene editing medicines for serious diseases.

Verve Therapeutics — VERVE-102 — Atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease — Phase 1b/2 Heart-2 study presented at European Atherosclerosis Society Congress demonstrated durable, dose-dependent lowering of PCSK9 and LDL via single-dose in vivo base editing.

Caribou Biosciences — vispa-cel and CB-011 — Hematologic malignancies — Management team to participate in fireside chat at H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual BioConnect Investor Conference on May 19, 2026, highlighting chRDNA genome-editing technology for armored cell therapies.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR in vivo pipeline — Serious diseases — Announced pricing of up to $319.4 million public offering to fund development of durable, precision in vivo gene editing medicines.

Verve Therapeutics — VERVE-102 — Atherosclerotic CVD prevention — Base editing mechanism achieved durable, dose-dependent LDL lowering; presented at European Atherosclerosis Society Congress and published in NEJM.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CRISPR/Cas9 and SyNTase™ gene editing platform commercialization — CASGEVY + diversified pipeline (hemoglobinopathies, cardiovascular, oncology) — Strategic collaboration with Vertex Pharmaceuticals; HQ in Zug, Switzerland with US R&D in Boston and San Francisco.

Caribou Biosciences — chRDNA genome-editing technology for off-the-shelf CAR-T therapies — vispa-cel + CB-011 — Clinical-stage biopharmaceutical focused on hematologic malignancies; HQ in Berkeley, California.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR commercialization strategy — In vivo gene editing pipeline — Exclusive licensee of Cas12a and Cas9 patent estates; raised $319.4M in public offering May 2026.

Verve Therapeutics — Development of single-dose in vivo base editing medicines — VERVE-102 — Heart-2 study data published in NEJM showing 88% PCSK9 reduction; positioned to address treatment persistence issues in familial hypercholesterolemia.

Caribou Biosciences — Clinical-stage CRISPR genome-editing development — vispa-cel and CB-011 — Utilizing chRDNA technology to create armored off-the-shelf CAR-T cell therapies for hematologic malignancies.

Editas Medicine — In vivo CRISPR medicine commercialization — Broad pipeline of transformative in vivo medicines — Exclusive licensee of Broad Institute's Cas12a patent estate and Broad Institute/Harvard University's Cas9 patent estates for human medicines.

Verve Therapeutics — In vivo base editing commercialization — VERVE-102 — Single-dose therapeutic candidate for heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia; Heart-2 study data confirms safety and efficacy profile.

CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing — Phase: Approved — Population: Sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia — Status: First approved CRISPR-based therapy globally.

vispacabtagene regedleucel (vispa-cel) — Mechanism: chRDNA genome-editing for armored CAR-T cells — Phase: Clinical — Population: Hematologic malignancies — Differentiation: Off-the-shelf therapy designed for broad access and rapid treatment.

Editas Medicine CRISPR programs — Mechanism: Cas9 and Cas12a genome editing — Phase: Preclinical to clinical pipeline — Population: Patients with serious diseases — Strategy: Development of durable, precision in vivo gene editing medicines.

VERVE-102 — Mechanism: In vivo base editing (CRISPR-derived) targeting PCSK9 gene — Phase: 1b/2 (Heart-2) — Population: Patients with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia or premature coronary artery disease — Comparator: None (dose-ranging) — Results: Up to 88% PCSK9 reduction, 62% LDL reduction; safety profile consistent with prior studies.

vispa-cel — Mechanism: chRDNA genome-editing for armored CAR-T cell therapy — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Patients with hematologic malignancies — Status: Off-the-shelf candidate under development.

CB-011 — Mechanism: chRDNA genome-editing for armored CAR-T cell therapy — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Patients with hematologic malignancies — Status: Off-the-shelf candidate under development.

VERVE-102 — Mechanism: In vivo base editing to mimic cardioprotective PCSK9 variants — Phase: Clinical (Heart-2 study) — Population: Patients with genetic high cholesterol or premature coronary disease — Endpoints: 88% PCSK9 reduction, 62% LDL reduction at highest dose.
CRISPR Therapeutics — Investor Relations — Bank of America Securities 2026 Global Healthcare Conference — Senior management presentation scheduled for May 12, 2026, 1:40 p.m. PDT.
Caribou Biosciences — Investor Relations — H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual BioConnect Investor Conference — Management team fireside chat scheduled for May 19, 2026, 12:30 p.m. ET in New York.
Editas Medicine — Public offering — Capital raise — Pricing of up to $319.4 million in equity offering announced May 26, 2026 to fund CRISPR-based drug development.
Editas Medicine — Public equity financing — Up to $319.4 million raised — Proceeds to support CRISPR-based in vivo medicine pipeline development.
